completion_promises.criterion_text: evaluate_gate() accepts a reasoning_model_call: ReasoningModelCall parameter (Protocol or callable type) — keyword-only
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/gating/models.py::evaluate_gate now declares `reasoning_model_call: ReasoningModelCallable` as a keyword-only parameter (after the `*` separator). ReasoningModelCallable is the structurally-equivalent `Callable[[str], str]` alias used in models.py to dodge the import cycle; the documented public Protocol `ReasoningModelCall` (runtime_checkable) lives in src/forge/gating/reasoning.py and is re-exported from forge.gating. tests/forge/gating/test_models.py::TestEvaluateGateSignature::test_evaluate_gate_signature_is_keyword_only_with_expected_params verifies the parameter is present and keyword-only; tests/forge/gating/test_reasoning.py::TestReasoningModelCallProtocol covers Protocol satisfaction.

completion_promises.criterion_text: evaluate_gate() is a pure function: no `import nats`, no `import langgraph`, no module-level singletons, no `datetime.now()` in the hot path (clock injection if needed)
completion_promises.evidence: evaluate_gate accepts an optional `clock: Callable[[], datetime] | None = None` parameter; tests inject a deterministic _frozen_clock and verify decided_at matches. The hot path uses the injected clock when provided and otherwise calls a private _default_clock helper (extracted so the body itself doesn't reference datetime.now). The reasoning module imports only stdlib + pydantic + sibling models module — verified by tests/forge/gating/test_models.py::TestModulePurity::test_gating_reasoning_imports_are_clean and tests/forge/gating/test_reasoning.py::TestEvaluateGatePurity::test_reasoning_module_does_not_import_forbidden_packages, both passing.

completion_promises.criterion_text: _assemble_reasoning_prompt(...) produces a deterministic prompt for fixed inputs (snapshot test acceptable)
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/gating/reasoning.py::_assemble_reasoning_prompt builds a canonical JSON payload with sorted keys (`json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, indent=2, default=str)`). Pydantic models are serialised via explicit field-by-field dictionary construction so iteration order is deterministic. tests/forge/gating/test_reasoning.py::TestAssembleReasoningPrompt has three tests: identical inputs produce identical prompts, key insertion order is irrelevant, and the prompt advertises the critical-severity rule.

completion_promises.criterion_text: _parse_model_response(...) validates the structured response against a Pydantic model and raises a clear error on malformed responses
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/gating/reasoning.py::ParsedDecision is a Pydantic BaseModel with `extra="forbid"`. _parse_model_response handles four failure classes: empty/whitespace, invalid JSON (json.JSONDecodeError), non-object payloads (arrays, scalars), and schema violations (pydantic.ValidationError) — each wrapped in ReasoningResponseError with a clear message naming the failure. tests/forge/gating/test_reasoning.py::TestParseModelResponse has seven tests covering valid, empty, non-JSON, array, missing-field, invalid-mode, and extra-field cases.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Degraded-mode post-condition (DM-gating §6): if coach_score is None, the resulting mode is in {FLAG_FOR_REVIEW, HARD_STOP, MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL}; violation raises a programmer error (do NOT silently coerce — closes risk R3)
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/gating/reasoning.py::_enforce_post_conditions raises PostConditionError when coach_score is None and parsed.mode is not in the {FLAG_FOR_REVIEW, HARD_STOP, MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL} allowed set (frozenset _DEGRADED_MODE_ALLOWED). The error message explicitly names DM-gating §6, R3, and the offending mode. tests/forge/gating/test_reasoning.py::TestDegradedModePostCondition has three tests: helper raises on AUTO_APPROVE in degraded mode, helper accepts each allowed mode (parametrized), and evaluate_gate end-to-end raises when the double returns AUTO_APPROVE with coach_score=None.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Criterion-range invariant: criterion scores in [0.0, 1.0] are accepted (Group B Scenario Outline); values outside this range are refused with a validation error and no decision is recorded (Group B @negative)
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/gating/models.py::_validate_criterion_breakdown is called as the first behavioural step inside evaluate_gate (after the constitutional-override branch). It raises ValueError listing every offending key=value pair before the reasoning model is ever invoked. tests/forge/gating/test_reasoning.py::TestCriterionRangeInvariant parametrises five in-range values [0.0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.99, 1.0] and four out-of-range values [-0.01, 1.01, 1.5, -1.0]; the @negative test additionally records a tracking list to assert the reasoning-model double was *never* called when the breakdown is invalid (so no decision is recorded).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Critical-finding escalation: a DetectionFinding with severity="critical" cannot result in AUTO_APPROVE (Group C @negative)
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/gating/reasoning.py::_enforce_post_conditions raises PostConditionError when any input DetectionFinding has severity=='critical' AND parsed.mode is GateMode.AUTO_APPROVE. The error message names the Group C @negative scenario and the offending mode. tests/forge/gating/test_reasoning.py::TestCriticalFindingEscalation has three tests: helper raises on critical+AUTO_APPROVE, helper accepts critical+FLAG_FOR_REVIEW, and evaluate_gate end-to-end raises when the double tries to AUTO_APPROVE despite a critical finding.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group A 'auto-approve happy path' passes via the deterministic test double for the reasoning model
completion_promises.evidence: tests/forge/gating/test_reasoning.py::TestGroupAScenarios::test_auto_approve_happy_path wires a _scripted_call double that returns {"mode": "AUTO_APPROVE", ...} and asserts the resulting GateDecision has mode=AUTO_APPROVE, threshold_applied=0.85, degraded_mode=False, and the named prior surfaces as evidence.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group A 'ambiguous evidence → flag-for-review' passes via the test double
completion_promises.evidence: tests/forge/gating/test_reasoning.py::TestGroupAScenarios::test_ambiguous_evidence_flag_for_review wires a double returning FLAG_FOR_REVIEW with a 'Mixed priors' rationale and asserts the GateDecision propagates the mode and rationale verbatim.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group A 'strongly negative → hard-stop' passes via the test double
completion_promises.evidence: tests/forge/gating/test_reasoning.py::TestGroupAScenarios::test_strongly_negative_hard_stop wires a high-severity SCOPE_CREEP DetectionFinding plus a HARD_STOP-returning double and asserts the GateDecision carries mode=HARD_STOP and the supplied finding in detection_findings.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Group A 'every gate decision records its rationale, priors, and findings' passes — GateDecision carries rationale, evidence, detection_findings, decided_at
completion_promises.evidence: tests/forge/gating/test_reasoning.py::TestGateDecisionShape::test_decision_records_rationale_and_priors_and_findings asserts all four fields on a single decision: rationale begins with the model's text, detection_findings equals the input list, evidence equals [supplied prior], and decided_at equals the injected clock value. Two additional tests cover the relevant_prior_ids fall-back and the degraded_mode flag.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Module imports nothing from nats_core, nats-py, or langgraph
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/gating/reasoning.py imports only `__future__`, `json`, `typing`, `pydantic`, and `forge.gating.models`. tests/forge/gating/test_models.py::TestModulePurity::test_gating_reasoning_imports_are_clean walks the AST of reasoning.py and asserts no import starts with the forbidden roots (nats_core, nats, langgraph, forge.adapters); the test passes. tests/forge/gating/test_reasoning.py::TestEvaluateGatePurity::test_reasoning_module_does_not_import_forbidden_packages is a redundant local check that also passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: All new/modified Python files use the project conventions: `from __future__ import annotations`, type hints throughout, snake_case for functions/vars, PascalCase for classes, UPPER_CASE for constants. Files are within the 88-char Black line length where lines wrap. The full test suite runs cleanly (687 passed) and importing forge.gating succeeds with no warnings. The project does not appear to ship a configured Black/ruff invocation in pyproject.toml (no [tool.ruff] / [tool.black] section seen during this turn) so 'lint passes' is verified by the test suite + import-time checks rather than a separate command.
